issues: Indentation errors in compute_signal method cause logic to be unreachable or incorrect.
compute_signal does not handle 'self.position' initialization or existence check safely.
compute_signal uses 'self.position' but no clear state management shown; may cause errors.
compute_signal returns 'RISK_OFF' on exit condition but allocation logic returns empty dict for 'RISK_OFF' which is correct as per spec (SELL = no position), so this is okay.
compute_signal uses correct adapter 'fetch_reddit_activity' and uses correct columns 'posts' and 'comments'.
Signal logic correctly triggers when activity > 1.5 * 7-day MA (50% increase).
Allocation logic correctly allocates 5% per signal to gaming hardware/publisher stocks (ATVI, EA, NVDA) capped at 20%.
Allocation logic returns empty dict on 'RISK_OFF' (SELL) and other signals (HOLD), which matches spec intent.
Overall logic intent matches spec but code indentation and state handling issues prevent correct execution.
